---
title: "Federal Office of Rural Health Policy Rural Designation"
categories:
  - Rurality
data_description: "The Federal Office of Rural Health Policy Rural Designation combines Rural-Urban Commuting Area (RUCA) codes and the Road Ruggedness Scale (RRS) to create a binary rural classification."
data_time_frame: "Year; 2025"
data_spatial_scale: "County, Census Tract, and ZIP Code approximations"
data_format: "Excel"
data_source: "Health Resources & Services Administration (HRSA)"
data_orig_repo: "[2025](https://www.hrsa.gov/rural-health/about-us/what-is-rural/data-files)"
data_living_atlas: "N/A"
data_details: |
  HRSA defines rural as the following:

  - Non-metropolitan counties
  - Outlying metropolitan counties with no population from an urban area of 50,000 or more people
  - Census tracts with RUCA codes 4-10 in metropolitan counties
  - Census tracts of at least 400 square miles in area with population density of 35 or fewer people per square mile with RUCA codes 2-3 in metropolitan counties
  - Census tracts with RRS 5 and RUCA codes 2-3 that are at least 20 square miles in area in metropolitan counties
format:
  html:
    template: ../../assets/data_catalog/data_template.html
---
